description_long: This is a dark-colored, twin-boom airplane with a central fuselage and two engines mounted on the leading edge of each wing. The aircraft features a glazed cockpit canopy positioned at the front of the central fuselage, with a propeller on each engine nacelle. The wings are straight and mid-mounted, connecting the central fuselage to the twin tail booms, which terminate in a horizontal stabilizer forming an H-tail configuration. The surface is smooth and appears metallic, with minimal visible markings except for a few roundels or insignia on the wings and booms. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retracted or not modeled in this representation.
description: A dark, twin-boom airplane with mid-mounted straight wings, dual propeller engines, and a glazed forward cockpit canopy.
description_view_0: Side view showing the twin booms, central fuselage, cockpit canopy, and both propeller engines.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the H-tail configuration and the alignment of the twin booms and horizontal stabilizer.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the twin booms, cockpit, and propellers from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the dual propellers, cockpit canopy, and the straight wings extending from the central fuselage.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles, connecting to twin tail booms.
attribute_engine_type: Twin piston engines, each with a single propeller mounted on the leading edge of the wings.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers connected by a horizontal stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; configuration indeterminate from images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit canopy located at the front of the central fuselage, likely single or tandem seat.
